commands.review_summary:
This PR changes the Control UI quick-settings CSS, test assertions, and changelog so Personal spans 9 columns, the Appearance/Automations stack spans 3, and the narrower two-column order puts Personal before the side cards.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the Personal card at span 8, the side stack at span 4, and the two-column breakpoint ordering Appearance before Personal; opening /config at the affected widths would exercise that path.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label and MEMBER authorship make this a maintainer review/landing item; there is no narrow automated repair task to queue.

target.title: fix(ui): widen settings personal card
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the quick-settings Personal card was too narrow next to Appearance/Automations, leaving the identity controls cramped.
- Why it matters: the settings page should match the intended 3/1 desktop split and keep the secondary settings grouped to the right.
- What changed: Personal now spans 9 of 12 columns, the Appearance/Automations side stack spans 3, and the responsive order keeps Personal before the side cards.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no config behavior, gateway behavior, auth, or settings persistence logic changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI settings layout now uses the requested 3/1 split for Personal versus Appearance/Automations.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Vite Control UI at `http://localhost:5173/config`, dev Gateway at `ws://127.0.0.1:19001`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started `pnpm ui:dev`, started `pnpm gateway:dev`, connected the browser to the dev Gateway, and opened `/config`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): browser-rendered settings page showed Personal as the wide left card with Appearance and Automations stacked on the right.
- Observed result after fix: the requested 3/1 layout rendered without overlapping controls at the desktop viewport.
- What was not tested: full cross-browser matrix and mobile device hardware.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): original screenshot showed Personal and Appearance using a less balanced split.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the quick-settings CSS allocated Personal `span 8` and the side stack `span 4`, and the tablet order placed Appearance before Personal.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the existing CSS assertion only checked generic span/order strings, not the intended 9/3 split.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/styles/config-quick.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Personal spans 9 columns, the side stack spans 3, and responsive ordering keeps Personal before Appearance/Automations.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is CSS-only layout behavior already covered by the quick-settings stylesheet assertions.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `config-quick styles`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI settings page uses a wider Personal card with Appearance and Automations stacked beside it on desktop.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Personal: 8 cols] [Appearance + Automations: 4 cols]

After:
[Personal: 9 cols] [Appearance + Automations: 3 cols]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI + local dev Gateway
- Relevant config (redacted): default dev profile; auth token redacted

### Steps

1. Open Control UI settings at `/config`.
2. Inspect the desktop quick-settings grid.
3. Run the focused CSS layout test.

### Expected

- Personal is the wide card; Appearance and Automations stack in the right column.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: desktop `/config` layout through local Vite Control UI connected to the dev Gateway; focused style regression test.
- Edge cases checked: responsive order assertions for Personal before Appearance/Automations.
- What you did **not** verify: full mobile hardware/browser matrix.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

None.

## Verification

- `git diff --check -- CHANGELOG.md ui/src/styles/config-quick.css ui/src/styles/config-quick.test.ts`
- `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22.22.2 >/dev/null && corepack pnpm test ui/src/styles/config-quick.test.ts`
target.author: bunsdev
